evals.prompt: I'm building an app with DUI. Create a settings form with fields for: display name, email address, and a theme preference toggle (light/dark/system). The form should have a save button and use proper layout components.
evals.expected_output: HTML/TS using dui-input, dui-toggle-group or dui-select for theme, dui-button, manual flex/grid styles for layout, --space tokens for spacing values, CSS custom properties for any styling overrides.
expectations: Uses dui-input for text fields rather than raw <input> elements
Uses dui-toggle-group or dui-select for the theme preference (not manually styled buttons)
Uses dui-button for the save action
Any styling overrides use CSS custom properties (--input-bg, --button-bg, etc.) rather than inline styles or shadow DOM manipulation
Uses semantic design tokens (--color-primary, --color-fg, etc.) rather than hardcoded color values
Does not use !important

evals.prompt: Create a confirmation dialog for deleting a user account. It should have a warning icon, a title, a description explaining the action is permanent, and Confirm/Cancel buttons. The confirm button should be styled as danger. Use DUI components.
evals.expected_output: HTML/TS using the dui-alert-dialog compound component family with proper hierarchy, ::part(root) or tokens for danger styling.
expectations: Uses dui-alert-dialog (not dui-dialog) for a confirmation flow

Maintains correct compound component hierarchy: dui-alert-dialog-trigger, dui-alert-dialog-popup, dui-alert-dialog-title, dui-alert-dialog-description, dui-alert-dialog-action, dui-alert-dialog-cancel all nested properly
Uses dui-icon for the warning icon with slot-based SVG content
Styles the confirm button as danger using the variant property or --button-bg/--button-fg tokens, not inline styles
Uses ::part(root) for any CSS properties not covered by tokens (e.g. box-shadow), rather than trying to pierce shadow DOM
Does not restructure the compound component hierarchy (e.g. pulling trigger outside alert-dialog)

evals.prompt: Build a dashboard layout with a collapsible sidebar on the left, a header bar with breadcrumbs, and a main content area showing 4 stat cards with a data table below them. Use DUI components throughout.
evals.expected_output: HTML/TS using dui-sidebar, dui-breadcrumb, dui-data-table, layout primitives, and semantic tokens.
expectations: Uses dui-sidebar with appropriate sub-components (dui-sidebar-header, dui-sidebar-content, dui-sidebar-menu, etc.)
Uses dui-breadcrumb with dui-breadcrumb-item children for navigation
Uses dui-data-table for the data table rather than a raw <table>
Uses dui-badge for stat card values/changes rather than custom styled spans
Uses semantic design tokens for colors rather than hardcoded values
Dark mode works via class="dark" on a parent, not manual color overrides

evals.prompt: The DUI inspector is available in this project. I want to add a separator between the two sections on the current page and change the primary button's border radius to fully rounded. Use the inspector to make these changes, then tell me what source file changes are needed.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses __dui_inspect() for discovery, __dui_mutate for changes, __dui_inspect to verify, and __dui_export() to get source file diffs.
expectations: Calls __dui_inspect() to discover existing components on the page before making changes
Uses __dui_mutate.insertComponent() to add the dui-separator
Uses __dui_mutate.setComponentToken() or __dui_mutate.setToken() for the border radius change (--button-radius or --radius-full)
Calls __dui_inspect(selector) after mutations to verify the changes took effect
Calls __dui_export() to get structured source file changes
Reports the export results as actionable file edits the user can apply

evals.prompt: Build a notifications panel for a DUI app. It should: show a list of notifications with status badges (read/unread/error), have a loading spinner while fetching, show a horizontal rule between each notification, include a 'Mark all read' button that pops up a small confirmation near the button (not a full modal), and show an empty state message when there are no notifications.
evals.expected_output: HTML/TS using dui-badge for status, dui-spinner for loading, dui-separator instead of hr, dui-popover for the small confirmation (not dui-dialog), and no custom markup where a DUI component exists.
expectations: Uses dui-badge for notification status indicators (read/unread/error) rather than custom styled spans
Uses dui-spinner for the loading state rather than a custom animation div
Uses dui-separator between notifications rather than <hr> or border divs
Uses dui-popover (not dui-dialog or dui-alert-dialog) for the small contextual confirmation near the button
Uses dui-button for the 'Mark all read' action
Does not build custom markup for things where a DUI component exists (spinner, badge, separator, progress, icon)
